matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules also receive the actual sample REF -> ALT change; curated allele-change rules take precedence when the observed ALT allele or exact change has a known direction.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a SIRT6 genome-maintenance and longevity thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of DNA repair, telomere maintenance, inflammation, glucose or lipid metabolism, or centenarian-enriched biology because SIRT6 regulates chromatin and stress resilience. This is an aging-biology prediction, not a guarantee of exceptional longevity.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: Observed rs350846 suggests this sample carries the SIRT6 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a SIRT6 genome-maintenance and longevity thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of DNA repair, telomere maintenance, inflammation, glucose or lipid metabolism, or centenarian-enriched biology because SIRT6 regulates chromatin and stress resilience. This is an aging-biology prediction, not a guarantee of exceptional longevity. This specific marker is curated as longevity-associated SIRT6 SNP. rs350846 is a common SIRT6 polymorphism curated here as a research-grade human longevity marker. The local database does not treat it as a diagnostic allele, but as a cohort-dependent aging association.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs350846 with the actual DNA change {change}. Anchor the SIRT6 prediction to that observed REF -> ALT result instead of treating the variant as only a theoretical marker. The current preview does not infer zygosity or allele dose, so dosage-specific conclusions require genotype fields.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Reported as a human longevity-associated marker at the SIRT6 locus.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: Observed centSIRT6-like N308K/A313S suggests a centenarian-enriched SIRT6 thesis: the sample may carry a rare functional background linked to stronger genome-maintenance activity in experimental follow-up.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched centSIRT6 (N308K/A313S) with the actual DNA change {change}. Anchor the SIRT6 prediction to that observed REF -> ALT result instead of treating the variant as only a theoretical marker. The current preview does not infer zygosity or allele dose, so dosage-specific conclusions require genotype fields.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Enhanced mono-ADP-ribosylase activity and stronger interaction with Lamin A/C were reported in functional studies.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a SIRT6 genome-maintenance and longevity thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of DNA repair, telomere maintenance, inflammation, glucose or lipid metabolism, or centenarian-enriched biology because SIRT6 regulates chromatin and stress resilience. This is an aging-biology prediction, not a guarantee of exceptional longevity. An observed SIRT6 variant places this sample in the curated SIRT6 research context. The bundled SIRT6 knowledge base is centered on aging and genome-maintenance biology. Most currently relevant human variants are interpreted as research-grade longevity or regulatory signals rather than as routine pathogenic clinical alleles. SIRT6 is a core longevity and genome-stability gene, so common and rare human variants are mainly interpreted through aging and cellular-maintenance phenotypes.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: SIRT6 is heavily studied in aging, genome stability, inflammation, lipid and glucose metabolism, and cardiovascular disease.
Rare centenarian-enriched alleles and selected common SNPs are being used to connect human genetics with SIRT6 functional biochemistry.
Aging and longevity research.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a SIRT6 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a SIRT6 genome-maintenance and longevity thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of DNA repair, telomere maintenance, inflammation, glucose or lipid metabolism, or centenarian-enriched biology because SIRT6 regulates chromatin and stress resilience. This is an aging-biology prediction, not a guarantee of exceptional longevity. SIRT6 methylation should be interpreted as chromatin and transcriptional context around an NAD-dependent longevity enzyme. Because SIRT6 function is closely tied to chromatin regulation, methylation can be informative, but not as a one-to-one biomarker. The bundled SIRT6 knowledge base is centered on aging and genome-maintenance biology. Most currently relevant human variants are interpreted as research-grade longevity or regulatory signals rather than as routine pathogenic clinical alleles. SIRT6 is a core longevity and genome-stability gene, so common and rare human variants are mainly interpreted through aging and cellular-maintenance phenotypes.
